PD scenario body: You are at work and find out that an opportunity for promotion in your department just came up. You think you might be eligible and decide to ask your boss for more details. Your boss tells you that the selection criteria are very strict and that your application will likely be rejected.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: The next day, you bump into your boss in the corridor. You then realize that although your boss thinks you may not be ready for promotion just yet, they strongly believe you are capable of being ready for one next year and are likely to succeed.
PD comprehension question: Your boss thinks you shouldn't apply for a promotion now because ...
PD correct response: You will be ready for one next year.

PD scenario body: You are working on a very important team project. The project deadline is soon, and your colleagues still haven't completed their portion, and you wonder if you should complete their part. You reach out to them, conveying your concerns about the deadline and workload, and they begin working in a timely manner.
PD comprehension question: Your colleagues are likely...
PD correct response: Grateful that you politely communicated your concerns over their contribution.
PD incorrect response: Annoyed that you're telling them what to do.

PD scenario body: Even though you normally excel at your job, you recently had a rough week at work. Specifically, you made a couple of mistakes on an important project. When you think about the future, you think it is likely that your job is secure.
PD comprehension question: In the upcoming weeks at work, you will probably ...

PD scenario body: You recently turned in a big project at work.  You decide to re-read your submitted work, and realize that you accidentally deleted a section you meant to include. Given that you accidentally made this deletion, you think that you are likely to receive positive feedback.
PD comprehension question: Are you going to get positive feedback on your project?

PD scenario body: You are about to give a presentation to your supervisor and coworkers. The material in the presentation has come from the research you have done in your job. During the presentation, you feel that your supervisor and coworkers will think you seem smart.
PD comprehension question: Will others think you seem intelligent when presenting?

PD scenario body: You are having an intellectual discussion with your colleague. On one of the questions they ask you, you are 90% certain that you know the correct answer. When you respond, you feel confident.
PD comprehension question: Are you uneasy about your answer?

PD scenario body: You are required to give a presentation to a group of work colleagues that you know well. They all are quiet during your presentation. As you think about it later at home, you think that your colleagues found your presentation interesting.
PD comprehension question: Your cohort members were probably quiet because ...
PD correct response: They were engaged by your presentation.
PD incorrect response: They were bored by your presentation.

PD scenario body: Your supervisor asks you to write a report. The finished document is quite brief but took a lot of time and effort. Based on your writing, you expect your supervisor's opinion of you will be positive.
PD comprehension question: Your supervisor probably found you to be...

PD scenario body: Your supervisor calls a meeting tied to a new initiative and asks each of you in turn to present your ideas and prior experience. When it is your turn to speak, you pause frequently. As you reflect on the meeting later in the day, you feel that the supervisor thought you sounded silly.
PD comprehension question: Your supervisor probably found you to be ...

PD scenario body: You recently turned in a report, and your boss asks to see you about your work. As you walk into their office, they tell you to sit down because there's lots of feedback to go through, and they look at you with a frown.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: During the meeting, you realize that your boss is taking great care to give you useful feedback and that they see this as a way to provide you with a valuable learning opportunity.
PD comprehension question: Your boss probably thinks that ...

PD scenario body: You have almost completed an important project. Part of your evaluation is determined by your coworkers. You know most of them well and feel that their opinions of you will make them evaluate you favorably.
PD comprehension question: Your coworkers will probably...

PD scenario body: You are being interviewed for a leadership position within your department. Before you walk into the room, you notice you feel shaky. You feel incredibly excited.
PD comprehension question: Does feeling shaky frighten you?

PD scenario body: Your supervisor asks you to write a report. The finished document is quite brief but took a lot of time and effort. Based on your writing, you expect your supervisor's opinion of you will be negative.
PD comprehension question: Your supervisor probably found you to be...

PD scenario body: You have been assigned a task, and you try very hard to make sure it is thorough. The job is quite difficult. When your supervisor sees it, you think they will judge your efforts as suitable.
PD comprehension question: Will your supervisor judge your efforts as reasonable?

PD scenario body: You heard a rumor your supervisor is planning on firing someone in your position. Although you are normally a good employee, you were late for a meeting earlier this week. You feel that the probability of you getting demoted is minimal.
PD comprehension question: You think that your supervisor could be ...

PD scenario body: You are the third and final candidate to be interviewed for a job. The interview panel greets you as you walk into the conference room. When you sit down, you feel your cheeks burning red, and you start to shake. The panel probably sees you as nervous.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: After the interview, you realize that it is normal to be nervous during panel interviews. The panelists probably saw you as reasonably calm and enthusiastic.
PD comprehension question: Do you think the interviewers see you as a nervous person?

PD scenario body: This morning you hear the news that a lot of companies are currently downsizing. You think about what will happen if you lose your job. You decide that finding another job will be possible.
PD comprehension question: If you lose your job, you will probably ...
PD correct response: Find a new job that you enjoy.

PD scenario body: It is your first day at a new job, and you report to your manager. As you approach them, you stumble, then recover. They noticed, and their first impression of you is probably that you are very prepared.
PD comprehension question: Your manager will probably think that ...
PD correct response: You are ready to take on challenges.

PD scenario body: You have started working on a new committee with other staff, and you are given a task that normally takes a few days, but you manage to finish it the same day. You show it to the rest of the committee, who find only one mistake in your work. As the meeting ends, you expect they think your work is commendable.
PD comprehension question: Was the committee pleased with your work?

PD scenario body: In the corridor at work, your boss asks you a question, and you realize you respond incorrectly. Your boss corrects you. You think the experience of being corrected by your boss is awful.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: In the end, you remind yourself that occasionally making mistakes does not make you a bad employee. Your boss had corrected you kindly, as was their job, and you feel that the whole interaction went well.
PD comprehension question: Is it okay to occasionally answer a question from your boss incorrectly?

PD scenario body: Your boss calls a meeting to discuss a new project that will involve most of the staff at your office. You are suddenly asked to contribute your ideas to the discussion. You quickly come up with some ideas on the spot and think that your colleagues will find your ideas to be exciting.
PD comprehension question: The people in your office will probably ...

PD scenario body: You have a big project due at the end of the week. Your work isn't finished, but you consider taking a break to go out with friends. Taking the night off would be reasonable.
PD comprehension question: Is it okay that you stopped working to go out with friends?

PD scenario body: You have to make a presentation about your work to your colleagues and your boss. As you start, you stumble on your first few words. Continuing with it, you think that your performance will be evaluated positively.
PD comprehension question: Do you think others will like your presentation?

PD scenario body: Your boss has hired a few interns for the summer. Your boss asks you to stay after work to give them a brief presentation about the university's goals and values but tells you that this is completely optional. You feel a little nervous about giving the presentation, and you decide to stay.
PD comprehension question: Do you stay to give the optional presentation?

PD scenario body: A coworker suggests that your team participates in a workshop on writing for the workplace. You go online and read information about the workshop. As you navigate through the site, the thought of other people looking at your writing makes you feel enthusiastic
PD comprehension question: Do you expect to feel uncomfortable if others look at your work?

PD scenario body: You have to give a presentation to your boss. As you think about the talk, you feel butterflies in your stomach. On the morning of the talk, you feel nervous, and you think your nerves will be manageable.
PD comprehension question: Are you able to tolerate your anxiety?

PD scenario body: You are working on a project you enjoy. However, you were just informed that during the next staff meeting you will have to give a five-minute oral presentation to the rest of the staff. On the evening before the presentation, you feel frightened about the idea of public speaking, and you decide your fear is tolerable.
PD comprehension question: Can you handle your fear of giving the oral presentation?

PD scenario body: You are in a meeting, and during a brief break, you call home to speak to your significant other. You are late getting back into the meeting room, so everyone looks up when you open the door. Others see your actions as disruptive.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: After the meeting you realize that the other students were probably just surprised that the door suddenly opened. Chances are they think that your opening and closing the door quietly means you are considerate.
PD comprehension question: Did people think your returning to the meeting late was rude?

PD scenario body: You are giving a speech at a convention for work. As you speak, you look into the crowd and notice that your boss’s expression is almost emotionless, but you can make out a slight frown.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: After the speech, you realize that people often look emotionless and frown when they are concentrating and focusing hard. You think that your boss was most likely feeling impressed.
PD comprehension question: Does your boss’s neutral expression mean that they are disappointed with your speech?

PD scenario body: You don't have stable internet access at home and you found out the job you're applying for is remote. You wonder how you'll be able to keep up with your work if you get the job. As you think about the situation, you feel stressed.
PD comprehension question: Are you worried about applying to the remote job?

PD scenario body: You are babysitting, and the child is outside playing with friends. You look out the window to check on them, and they are not there. The child is probably fine.
PD comprehension question: Is the child okay?

PD scenario body: You recently saw on the news that the economy has been particularly bad. Even though your boss typically gives you good reviews, you wonder whether your job is safe. You think you will likely be fine.
PD comprehension question: Is it likely that you will be fired?

PD scenario body: The window for staff evaluations just closed, and you are not sure whether you did well or not. You feel nervous as you think about how other people graded you. You think being nervous is manageable.
PD comprehension question: Can you tolerate your nervousness as you wait to find out your evaluation?

PD scenario body: You are having a conversation with your supervisor in their office. You notice that you are stuttering over your words a bit more than you normally do. As the conversation continues, you decide this is okay.
PD comprehension question: Are you embarrassed due to stuttering over your words?

PD scenario body: You are giving a presentation at work. During one of your slides, you lose your train of thought and have to take a moment to look over your notes. As you start again, you think that losing one's train of thought while presenting is normal.
PD comprehension question: Is it normal to lose your train of thought while presenting?

PD scenario body: Although you are normally on time for work events, today you receive an email from your boss saying that you are a few minutes late for a scheduled meeting with them, which you had completely forgotten about. As you quickly head to the office, you wonder whether they are going to judge you for being late. As you walk into the room, your anxiety is tolerable.
PD comprehension question: During the meeting, your boss will probably think you are ...
PD correct response: Competent, even though you made a mistake.

PD scenario body: Some important people are visiting your office, and you are asked to present a project to them. On the day of the presentation, you arrange your slides and mentally prepare yourself. You think the visitors will evaluate your performance as organized.
PD comprehension question: Will the visitors be unimpressed with your performance?

PD scenario body: You have to turn in an important project today. When you open your computer, you can't find the file anywhere. The project must have been deleted.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: You take a deep breath and decide to tell your boss about what happened. You feel ok.
PD comprehension question: Are you anxious because your project got deleted?

PD scenario body: You are assigned a project to complete for work. You've been grouped with coworkers who usually don't contribute much to work projects. You start to think about how the project will go, and feel uneasy.
PD comprehension question: Are you worried about completing the presentation?

PD scenario body: You and your extended family have very different political and religious beliefs. This sometimes causes tension, but you make a strong effort to have a good relationship with your family.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: As time goes on, you do not expect your family to drift apart, and you think that your family will become closer.
PD comprehension question: Will your family's relationships become more strained as time goes on?

PD scenario body: You are preparing to meet with your boss for a performance review. You heard from other people that your boss is in an incredibly bad mood today. You worked hard this semester and think it is likely you will get a good review.
PD comprehension question: Will you probably get a good review?

PD scenario body: You have a one-on-one meeting today with your intimidating boss, which you are nervous about. During the meeting, you momentarily lose your train of thought. After that, your anxiety is tolerable.
PD comprehension question: Are you able to tolerate your anxiety while meeting with your boss?

PD scenario body: The morning of your first evaluation with your new boss has arrived. They tell you that they have strong views about your work. You think that they likely find your work excellent.
PD comprehension question: Do you think your new boss has an unfavorable opinion of your work?

PD scenario body: You have an important presentation today. You've been working hard on it because it is a requirement for your job. When you begin the presentation, you feel prepared.
PD comprehension question: Do you feel ready for your presentation today?

PD scenario body: Today, you will receive an important email. It will tell you whether you received or didn't get a promotion that would really advance your career.  As you think about waiting for the email, you feel excited.
PD comprehension question: Are you miserable waiting for the important email?

PD scenario body: Today, you will receive an important email. It will tell you whether you received or didn't get a promotion that would really advance your career.  As you think about waiting for the email, you feel dread.
PD comprehension question: Are you miserable waiting for the important email?

PD scenario body: A friend suggests that you join a creative writing organization. He tells you that members of the organization will read and critique your writing. As you listen to your friend, the thought of other people looking at your writing makes you feel enthusiastic.
PD comprehension question: Do you expect to feel uncomfortable if others look at your work?

PD scenario body: Today you are receiving an email about whether or not you received a promotion. You have a few hours to wait until you'll get the email. When you think about waiting that long, you feel miserable.
PD comprehension question: Do you feel OK having to wait for the email?

PD scenario body: Your boss calls on you to answer a question about the plans you just discussed. You don't know what to say. Your mind goes blank.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: You give yourself a moment to collect your thoughts and then answer the question. When you think about the answer you gave, you feel good.
PD comprehension question: Are you able to answer the question?

PD scenario body: You prepared a lot for an important presentation with your colleagues. Your section of the presentation didn't go as well as you expected. You think about whether you will be able to do better in your next section, and realize this was your last section.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: Right now you aren't happy, but you think about your group's overall presentation. You realize that it was good.
PD comprehension question: Did your presentation go poorly?

PD scenario body: You have a project due at work next week. You haven't started it yet because you're unsure how to best complete it. When you think about completing the project on time, you feel confident.
PD comprehension question: Are you worried about completing your project on time?

PD scenario body: You recently replaced your laptop and had to go with a cheaper option. Your boss asks you to complete a project that will require an application that your new computer doesn’t have. When you think about completing your work for this project, you feel capable.
PD comprehension question: Do you feel capable of completing the work for this project?

PD scenario body: Your neighbors were really loud last night so you couldn't sleep. You have a huge presentation to give to your coworkers this morning on a complicated topic. As you think about how you'll do in the presentation, you feel prepared.
PD comprehension question: Are you ready for your presentation?

PD scenario body: You are proposing a new idea in a meeting today. You only have an hour before the meeting and you don't know where to start. When you think about your ability to complete the proposal, you feel doubtful.
PD comprehension question: Will you be able to finish your proposal in time?

PD scenario body: You've decided to accept a job in another department. All of your close friends are applying to committees within your current department. When you think about your decision to switch departments, you feel satisfied.
PD comprehension question: Are you happy with your decision to switch departments?

PD scenario body: You are walking to an important meeting with your supervisor. Suddenly your mind goes blank as you reach for the doorknob to her office. You think about your mind going blank and think that this is normal.
PD comprehension question: Is it scary that your mind went blank right before your meeting?

PD scenario body: You have a lot of work to do for your job's evaluation. One of your most important tasks is to make a well-prepared, lengthy presentation to your coworkers. As you imagine yourself giving your presentation, you feel excited.
PD comprehension question: Are you looking forward to giving your presentation to your coworkers?

PD scenario body: You are having a debate with coworkers about a task at your job. The other people have more experience than you do. When you think about arguing your position, you feel confident.
PD comprehension question: Are you worried about the debate?

PD scenario body: You have a big presentation to your boss and other staff members. You know everyone's eyes are on you as you stand in front of the room. You feel nervous.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: You give yourself a moment to collect your thoughts and begin the presentation. During the presentation, you feel prepared.
PD comprehension question: Are you feeling prepared for your presentation?

PD scenario body: Your boss asks a question to the group in your virtual meeting. You click the "raise hand" button and prepare your response. You think that you'll sound dumb.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: You get called on and say your answer. Everyone probably thought you sounded knowledgeable
PD comprehension question: Did the other people think you sounded dumb?

PD scenario body: You are giving a presentation at a meeting tomorrow. You know that the quality of your presentation will be taken into account by your boss. As you think about the presentation, you feel prepared.
PD comprehension question: Are you prepared for your presentation?

PD scenario body: You and your partner share a computer. You have a work assignment due tonight but they're using the computer. When you think about completing the work on time, you feel confident.
PD comprehension question: Will you get your assignment done in time?

PD scenario body: You wake up and realize that you're late for your first online meeting of the day. You log into the meeting and when you do, your supervisor stops talking. You apologize for being late in the chat, and spend the rest of the meeting feeling calm.
PD comprehension question: Are you anxious for the rest of the meeting because you were late?

PD scenario body: Your boss is watching your presentation in your meeting today. During the presentation, you stumble over your words a lot. You feel defeated.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: While you feel upset, you realize that it's natural to make mistakes. You think that your boss won't remember.
PD comprehension question: Is it okay that you made a mistake in front of your boss?

PD scenario body: Your partner wants you to take on a specific job. You would rather work in a different place but your partner has made it clear they are not on board with the job choice. When you think about talking with your partner about your choice, you feel prepared.
PD comprehension question: Can you handle talking to your partner about your job choice?

PD scenario body: You want to talk to someone about your plans for the future, but don't want to talk to your family about it. You think about other close people in your life, and decide to talk to your closest friend. When you think about their reaction, you think they will probably be encouraging.
PD comprehension question: Do you feel worried about talking to your friends about your plans for the future?

PD scenario body: You just received your performance evaluation, and you noticed their are some critiques about your work that you did not realize were an issue. While waiting for your meeting with your boss, you feel ready.
PD comprehension question: Are you spending your time worrying about meeting with your boss?

PD scenario body: You just sent an important email. As you reread what you've sent, you see a typo in the first sentence. You believe that you should be concerned.
PD comprehension question: Should you be worried about the typo?

PD scenario body: A new supervisor is hired for the department you are in, and you hear that he is very precise on how things are to be done. You meet him for the first time and talk about the upcoming month. Getting up to leave at the end of your meeting, you think that he found you to be intelligent.
PD comprehension question: Does your new supervisor have a good opinion of you?

PD scenario body: You are about to do your first online interview for an important promotion. You click the link when it is time to join the meeting,  but your internet cuts out. You think about the interviewers assuming you are late and feel nauseous.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: After a minute, your Internet comes back. You log back in and realize you feel more confident.
PD comprehension question: Do you feel sick throughout the rest of your interview?

PD scenario body: You check online to see if you were selected for a raise. You see that you were not, although you expected to be. You think about what this will mean for your future, and know everything will be fine.
PD comprehension question: Will there be terrible consequences for not getting this raise?

PD scenario body: The year just ended and your boss wants to meet with you about your performance. They have high expectations for you, but you performed worse than previous years. Your boss will be upset.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: You think about why they want you to perform well. You know it is because they want you to do well, so that makes you feel better.
PD comprehension question: Do you stay upset when thinking about talking to your boss about your performance this year?

PD scenario body: During a staff meeting, you are called on to share your thoughts. As you start to talk, you hear a loud burst of laughter behind you. Your fellow coworkers are laughing at a joke.
PD comprehension question: Are your coworkers laughing at you?

PD scenario body: Tonight, you find out the results of your yearly performance evaluation at work. Your rankings are low in one category, and you know your supervisor will want to meet with you to discuss your performance. When you think about what your supervisor will say to you, you feel ok.
PD comprehension question: Can you handle your department head's reaction to your poor evaluation?

PD scenario body: A new office initiative started by you and a few other coworkers was rejected by your boss and you have to tell the news to the others. They don't say anything when you tell them. They are probably feeling disappointed.
PD comprehension question: Are your coworkers disappointed that your initiative was rejected?

PD scenario body: You and some coworkers were given some extra tasks to complete by the end of the week, and you've been working hard. You overheard your coworkers saying that you'd be "crazy" to enjoy the extra work. You think about how productive it makes you feel to be working this hard, and feel excited.
PD comprehension question: Are you looking forward to completing the extra tasks?

PD scenario body: In your meeting today, you have an important presentation. In order to follow your boss's instructions, you must present for 15 minutes. When you think about completing this presentation, you feel prepared.
PD comprehension question: Are you able to complete the presentation?

PD scenario body: This year, you've decided to take on more weekly hours. This is your first time working this many hours and you've heard from your peers that it's difficult. When you think about the workload, you think that you will be able to handle it well.
PD comprehension question: Will you succeed with your new workload?

PD scenario body: You sent an email to your department a few hours ago, asking for responses on a topic. You check to see who's responded and notice that your boss hasn't responded yet. You think about how your boss didn't read the email from you and you feel sad.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: You look at who did respond, and notice other coworkers have given their input. When you think about your boss not responding yet, you are accepting.
PD comprehension question: Is it okay that your boss hasn't responded yet?

PD scenario body: Today, you will receive an important call. You'll find out if you got the job you interviewed for. As you think about waiting for the call, your anxiety is tolerable.
PD comprehension question: Are you able to tolerate feeling anxious while waiting for the call?

PD scenario body: You have a presentation for your department coming up, and you’ve been working hard in the hopes that you'll impress your supervisor. The night before, you find out a friend you had dinner with has a virus, and now you're feeling sick. You think your supervisor will understand and help you arrange a better time to present.
PD comprehension question: Will your supervisor penalize you for getting sick?

PD scenario body: As you're finishing an assignment at midnight, you realize you still have a couple of tasks left. You plan to wake up a little early to finish them. While you're setting your alarm and thinking about your plan, you feel confident.
PD comprehension question: Are you anxious about your plan to finish your tasks in the morning?

PD scenario body: You have a lot of tasks to finish tomorrow. You've been procrastinating getting it done because it is going to be hard. When you think about doing your work tonight, you feel ready.
PD comprehension question: Are you going to complete your work?

PD scenario body: You've decided to take time off work to travel. You don't know anyone who has taken this type of break, and all of your friends are questioning it. When you think about your decision, you feel like it's right.
PD comprehension question: Are you satisfied with your decision to go take time off to travel?

PD scenario body: You have had a lot of work pile up these past couple of weeks. As a result, you have not been able to spend time with friends or family as much as you would want and have often times felt lonely. Once your workload lessens, you plan on reconnecting with people who mean a lot to you.
PD comprehension question: Will you be able to hang out with friends and family when you have more time?

PD scenario body: You just started at a new job and you work with lots of new people. You hear a few of them laughing together and talking about going out to dinner and you wait for them to invite you, eager to make friends, but they don't. You feel okay with this and look forward to reaching out next time.
PD comprehension question: Is it too late to befriend your new coworkers?

PD scenario body: You recently decided to join a new neighborhood organization on top of your job. At a meeting for the new group, you brought the wrong notebook and have notes for your job, which aren't useful here. You think managing being in this new group will be difficult.
PD comprehension question: Will it be hard to be in this new organization?

PD scenario body: Work has been piling up at your job recently, and you were going to take the weekend to catch up. However, your friend reminded you of an event this weekend that you committed to, and it would be rude to cancel last minute. You think that completing your work will be doable.
PD comprehension question: Will you be able to do work this weekend?

PD scenario body: You are in a meeting and reach to put your phone away as the new head of your department starts talking. He mistakes this for you texting during the meeting and calls you out in front of everyone. You go up to apologize afterwards and you can't read your head of department's expression.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: After explaining what happened to the head of your department, he says he understands. You feel like you will still be able make a good impression.
PD comprehension question: Will your department head view you negatively because of this?

PD scenario body: You were very excited to return to in-person office work and decided to commit to a lot of activities. You've found it challenging to meet all of your responsibilities, and you worry that you are failing at everything.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: You realize that being back in-person is an adjustment, and it will take some time to get used to the business of an in-person workplace. You think that with everything you have going on, you're doing a great job.
PD comprehension question: Can you handle your responsibilities?

PD scenario body: You have started to volunteer at a local organization and have had a difficult time managing it with work. One of the volunteer leaders asked if you would be willing to help her out with a project outside of your usual responsibilities,and you are worried if she will be disappointed if you say no. After thinking on it, you tell her that you can't help out with this project because of work, but are willing to help out on the next one.
PD comprehension question: Is it okay to turn down additional responsibility?

PD scenario body: You've been very busy the past few months, and you haven't had a lot of free time. You finally have a chance to rest, but you feel guilty that you're taking a break rather than completing work. You think that you deserve to relax.
PD comprehension question: Should you do your work right now?

PD scenario body: You have enrolled in a data science program offered by your workplace because you think it will be a useful skill to acquire going forward. You know the demand for people with data science experience is increasing, but the course is very difficult. Imagining how you will do in the program, you believe your efforts to learn some data science skills will be futile.
PD comprehension question: You will most likely
PD correct response: Quit this program because you are doing poorly.
PD incorrect response: Complete the program and learn some useful data science skills.

PD scenario body: Your boss is talking about one of your role models in your field of work during a meeting, and she asks if anyone knows about their work. You quickly raise your hand in front of everyone, and your colleagues turn to you. You realize that you feel confident.
PD comprehension question: Are you embarrassed that you raised your hand?

PD scenario body: You would like to apply for a higher position at your job and further your skills. You know that the process of getting selected is rigorous, but you feel very passionate about achieving this goal and have worked hard to make yourself a good employee. You feel that your chances of getting this new job are reasonable.
PD comprehension question: You think that your application
PD correct response: Puts you in a good position to get this new job.
PD incorrect response: Will not be good enough to get you the job.

PD scenario body: You started working at a new job this fall. Since your job is remote, you haven't been able to meet any of your colleagues in person. You have seen photos of some of them hanging out, and you feel lonely.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: You decide to take initiative and ask for their numbers. You feel hopeful.
PD comprehension question: Can you still make friends with your colleagues even though everything is online?

PD scenario body: You have submitted a resume and an application for a job that you have been hoping to get. You hope to get an interview for the job after they review your application, but you know it is very competitive. With your qualifications, you expect to get a call.
PD comprehension question: Your qualifications will
PD correct response: Put you in a good position to get an interview.
PD incorrect response: Are probably not strong enough to make you stand out.

PD scenario body: You have been called in for a performance review with your boss. Overall, you do good work, but you know your boss has noticed some errors recently. As you prepare for the review, you think that your meeting will probably focus on your successes.
PD comprehension question: Your boss likely feels that
PD correct response: Most of your work has been of very high quality.
PD incorrect response: Your mistakes have been serious and unacceptable.

PD scenario body: You are currently have a lot going on with work. Your friend who you have not seen in a while has asked you to hang out the night before a deadline. You know that you need to do prioritize your work but worry that your friend will be upset for not making time for them.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: You realize that you will have greater flexibility in the next few weeks to see your friend and that they will understand that you need to work.
PD comprehension question: Will your friend be mad at you for not seeing them?

PD scenario body: You are preparing to apply for a new job. You have a meeting with a potential employer tomorrow, and you know that a good resume will be an important factor. As you look over your resume, you think that the interviewer will be favorable.
PD comprehension question: Your potential employer will probably think
PD correct response: That your previous experience is a good fit for the job.
PD incorrect response: That your listed skills are unimpressive.

PD scenario body: You are preparing to apply for a new job. You have a meeting with a potential employer tomorrow, and you know that a good resume will be an important factor.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: As you look over your resume, you do not anticipate your potential employer writing you off, and you think that the interviewer will be impressed.

PD comprehension question: Were they laughing at you?

PD scenario body: You are invited to a Halloween get-together and will only know a couple of people there. You decide to wear a really elaborate costume. When you arrive, everyone looks at you and probably think that you are creative.
PD comprehension question: Are the people there negatively judging your costume?

PD scenario body: You are sitting at a coffee shop when you see your friend. They walk past you and sit down next to someone else. They must have been distracted.
PD comprehension question: Is your friend upset with you?

PD scenario body: You meet an attractive new coworker during a meeting. After work, they request to follow you on social media. Before you let them follow you, you look over your own feed and feel confident.
PD comprehension question: Are you worried about what the other person thinks of your feed?

PD scenario body: You post a short video. Your friend comments something really embarrassing. Your followers will think that you and your friend are close.
PD comprehension question: Do others judge you for your friend's comment on your post?

PD scenario body: You arrive to a morning meeting exactly on time, but you are the last one to arrive. Your coworkers look at you as you pull out your chair, and it squeaks loudly. They all turn to look at you with a slight frown.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: In the end, you realize that your coworkers are just good employees who were enthusiastic to get the meeting started. They responded to you during the rest of the meeting quite positively.
PD comprehension question: Do your coworkers judge you negatively?

PD scenario body: You run to a meeting at work because you think you might be late. You make it to the meeting room on time, but you are breathing heavily and feel embarrassed because people are looking at you. As you sit down, to calm yourself, you decide to focus on your breath.
PD comprehension question: Does observing your breathing make you more anxious?

PD scenario body: You got the flu shot this season to protect yourself and others from the flu. Your boss, on the other hand, has been telling you about the parties they've frequently attended during this flu season despite not being vaccinated. You want to encourage them to either get the flu shot or wear a mask to work, but you think it will start a fight.
PD comprehension question: Are you worried that suggesting to your boss that they wear a mask or get the flu shot will cause problems?

PD scenario body: During your department meeting over Zoom, you're placed into breakout rooms. When you start the discussion, your camera is on but everyone else leaves their cameras off and their mics muted. You think that the others will think that you are ready.
PD comprehension question: Are the others in the meeting judging you?

PD scenario body: You get your evaluation back today and your results are lower than you expected. Your peers are talking about their results and they are more positive than yours. If you tell your peers about your evaluation results, they will be understanding.
PD comprehension question: Will your peers judge you for getting more negative evaluation results than them?

PD scenario body: You need to save money so you always bring meals from home. When you eat with your friends, you notice that they all go out to eat every lunch. You realize that you have a different situation than your friends and that's ok.
PD comprehension question: Are you hard on yourself because you don't buy lunch like your friends?

PD scenario body: Your grandchild wants to go trick-or-treating, and they begged you to dress up with them. You wear a costume and run into some people from work who aren't dressed up. They think your costume is awesome.
PD comprehension question: Do the people from work think you're lame for going trick-or-treating?

PD scenario body: You're out to lunch with a group of your friends. You make a joke that you think is really funny, but no one really laughs. They were probably distracted.
PD comprehension question: Did you say the wrong thing?

PD scenario body: Your family doesn't do sit-down dinners together. Your friend invites you to join her family for dinner, and when you arrive, everyone sits at the table and starts asking questions about each other's days. When it's your turn, you're not sure how to respond, but after you say something, you feel calm
PD comprehension question: Are you able to stay calm even though you don't know what to say?

PD scenario body: You are giving a presentation during your evening meeting. As you talk, you hear a few of your coworkers laughing in the corner. You look over at them and realize they were probably laughing at you.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: After a moment, you remember situations where you and your coworkers have laughed about things unrelated to who is speaking. You move on from their laughter and complete your presentation successfully.
PD comprehension question: Were your coworkers laughing at you?

PD scenario body: You used your phone to send a photo to a friend, and you can see that they opened it but didn't respond to you. You've seen them posting other things online, so they're definitely on their phone. Your friend probably didn't respond because they forgot.
PD comprehension question: Is your friend ignoring you on purpose?

PD scenario body: You work in a large office with your supervisors. One morning you have to call your father and discuss an important matter. Everyone else is working quietly, and several colleagues look up and seem annoyed.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: After you hang up, you realize that although everyone could hear your conversation, it was a short call and you were being as quiet as possible. You believe that your coworkers probably think calling your father from work means you are caring.
PD comprehension question: Were others judging you negatively because you called your father from work?

PD scenario body: Your partner invites a few friends over for the evening, and you both agreed that everyone would wear masks. When their friends arrive, they don't have masks and your partner doesn't say anything to them. You pull your partner aside and ask them to speak to their friends.
PD comprehension question: Do you feel comfortable talking to your partner?

PD scenario body: You notice your home is smaller and not as spacious as the homes of others that you know. Your coworkers have never been to your place, but want to come over. You think about their reaction to your home and they will be respectful.
PD comprehension question: Will your coworkers judge your home?

MTM Title: Sharing Social Media with New Friend
PD scenario body: You meet a new coworker and they ask you for your email so they can reach out to make plans. When you tell them you rarely get emails, they grow quiet. They must think that you are different, and that's alright.
PD comprehension question: Is it okay that your coworker thinks you're different?

PD scenario body: You invite a coworker over for dinner one night and you're in charge of the food. You aren't the best cook and you might embarrass yourself in front of your coworker. As your coworker arrives, you are calm.
PD comprehension question: Are you anxious as your coworker arrives?

PD scenario body: You and some friends are watching a movie at your home. At the end of the movie, your friend asks what that funny smell is. You know that's your dinner, and when you think about how to answer you feel hungry.
PD comprehension question: Are you anxious about telling your friend that the smell is your dinner?

PD scenario body: You have been invited to a friend's house for a holiday and you want to bring a gift, but can't afford to buy one. You decide to write a poem and recite it for their family. As you picture yourself reading it, you feel  excited.
PD comprehension question: Are you worried about reciting a poem for your friend's family?

PD scenario body: You recently downloaded a social media app, and when you look at your score compared to other people you know, you notice that yours is really low. If anyone sees your score, they will know that you don't use the app as much as them. This makes you feel unbothered.
PD comprehension question: Do you care that people know you don't use this app very much?

PD scenario body: You are in the car with your mother-in-law and you put on the radio. As you discuss which station to listen to, you realize that you and your mother-in-law have musical tastes that are wildly clashing.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: After having a discussion about the pros and cons of different music styles, you realize that she probably does not mind that your tastes clash and simply sees you as knowledgeable.
PD comprehension question: Does your mother-in-law think you know nothing about music?

PD scenario body: You are giving a presentation at a meeting and accidentally skip a section so have to start again. You are embarrassed by the mistake and worry what the audience thought of you. The audience probably thinks your delivery after starting over was awkward.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: Later that day, you realize that it is common to make mistakes when presenting. The audience probably thought your delivery after starting over was admirable.
PD comprehension question: Do you think that the audience thought of you as awkward for making a mistake?

PD scenario body: You are trying to think of a good joke to put in the next email you send out to your coworkers. You find a really funny one on a website after doing a quick search, but this is the first time you're ending your emails with a joke. Your coworkers will think that it's funny.
PD comprehension question: Do your coworkers like your joke?

PD scenario body: You are put in charge of your office's fundraising efforts and quickly realize that your coworkers are not very interested in the fundraising. At a meeting, you give a presentation about the importance of helping with the fundraising, during which your coworkers remain quiet. As you sit down after your presentation, you think they seemed bored.
PD comprehension question: The coworkers are probably thinking ...
PD correct response: That they don't want to help you with the fundraiser.
PD incorrect response: That they want to help you with the fundraiser.

PD scenario body: A friend is staying over at your house for a bit, and you realize they often ignore their chores. One day, you come home to see a pile of their dirty dishes in the sink while they have already left to go out. You decide to call them to settle this issue, and you feel reassured.
PD comprehension question: Did calling them make you feel more at ease?

PD scenario body: You just received an award for a project you worked on. You’ve only received congratulations from a few people, and you thought you’d have more reactions by now. You wonder if people aren’t congratulating you because they don’t think you deserve it.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: You remember why you worked on the project in the first place. After you think about it, you appreciate how hard you worked, and you feel better.
PD comprehension question: Do you feel worried about what other people think?

PD scenario body: You are trying come up with a closing for an email to send to your coworkers. You come up with a joke that your closest friends will love. Your coworkers will think it's funny.
PD comprehension question: Are you worried what your followers will think of your joke?

PD scenario body: You have a lot of unread emails. When you check your inbox today, you notice an explicit message from an email address you don't know. You decide to block the sender and feel more relaxed.
PD comprehension question: Do you get anxious about the email you received?

PD scenario body: You invite your supervisor to an event this weekend. You're the only one of your friends that's close with their supervisor and are unsure of how others will perceive you. As you get ready for the event, you feel happy.
PD comprehension question: Are you happy to have your supervisor come to your school event?

PD scenario body: You sent a text to a good friend. A day goes by, and they still have not replied. You think about how your friend doesn't use their phone often, and you feel better.
PD comprehension question: Is it okay that your friend hasn't responded yet?

PD scenario body: You're talking to a coworker about a presentation you're working on, and they seem really interested. They ask if they can look at it, and you agree. Before you send the slides to them, you look over it and feel good.
PD comprehension question: Are you worried about what your coworker thinks of your presentation?

PD scenario body: As you scroll through your feed on social media, you see a screenshot of your friends that shows them having a great time together over video chat. You weren't invited to hang out with them online. You think about it and decide that you can cope.
PD comprehension question: Can you handle that sometimes your friends hang out without you?

PD scenario body: You decide to come up with a creative post that is very different from what you usually post. You post something new and do not get any responses yet. When you think about the challenge of making creative content, you feel excited.
PD comprehension question: Do you let the challenge of making creative posts hold you back?

PD scenario body: You have been asked to give a presentation about some of your latest projects at a major conference. This is a great opportunity to highlight the good work you have been doing, but you feel nervous talking in front of such a big group of your peers. As you think about how the people at the conference will react to your presentation, you believe they will think you looked nervous.
PD comprehension question: After your presentation, people in the audience will probably think about
PD correct response: How much trouble you had giving the talk.
PD incorrect response: How well you did in your talk.

PD scenario body: There's an important question that you've been wanting to ask your friend for a while, but you haven't. You picture yourself asking when you meet next. As you think about the question, you feel ready.
PD comprehension question: Are you looking forward to asking your friend a question?

PD scenario body: A good friend of yours told you they'd meet you at your favorite restaurant for dinner one night. You text them that morning to ask what time to meet, but they haven't answered by early afternoon. You're quite upset at first, but then you realize they are probably just busy at work.
PD comprehension question: Did your friend forget about you?

PD scenario body: You see from social media stories that a group of your colleagues are hanging out together. You weren't invited, and it looks like they're having a great time. Your colleagues probably didn't invite you because they forgot.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: You are sad that you weren't invited to hang out with your colleagues. Your response to this situation is normal.
PD comprehension question: Did your colleagues exclude you on purpose?

PD scenario body: You are at a party with your partner and they are intoxicated. When you ask them if they'd like to go home, they say no and walk away from you. You think that they are having fun, so you decide to keep calm.
PD comprehension question: Did you end up staying relaxed?

PD scenario body: You made plans with your friends who you have been looking forward to seeing. When you drive to their house and call to tell them you're there, the call ends suddenly. You think it must have been an accidental hang-up or a dropped call, so you feel fine.
PD comprehension question: Did your friend mean to hang up on you?

PD scenario body: You're in a big group of your friends. Everyone is sharing stories, but you haven't said anything the whole conversation. Your friends probably think you're boring.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: While listening to your friends, you remember that your friend was quiet the other day. You decide that not always sharing is normal.
PD comprehension question: Is it normal to not share all of the time?

PD scenario body: Your partner takes you out to dinner. They mention something that upsets you, but you worry about confronting them in public. Ultimately, you decide to tell them what they said upset you, and they apologize genuinely.
PD comprehension question: Was your partner angry with you for voicing your concern?

PD scenario body: It's Saturday night, and you are at home. You hear your neighbors having a party and laughing on the porch, and you feel sad since you don't have plans or friends to be with tonight. You remember that there will be many other chances to see your friends, and you feel happy to be relaxing at home.
PD comprehension question: Will all of your weekends be spent like this?

PD scenario body: In between appointments, you have time to grab lunch at a cafe that's on the way. When you walk in, you see that most people are sitting with a friend, but you don't have anybody to eat with. You decide that it's normal to eat alone, and you end up enjoying your meal.
PD comprehension question: Does eating alone make you upset?

PD scenario body: While at a friend's house for a dinner party, your host mentions that you are a good piano player, and asks you to play something on the piano for the other guests. You know a few pieces you can play well, but you feel nervous playing on the spot without practice. Walking up to the piano, you think to yourself, these people are going to be disappointed.
PD comprehension question: When you finish playing, you expect the other guests to

PD scenario body: It is a long weekend, and you are invited to a friend's wedding. When you arrive to the venue, you notice that many people have brought a plus one, and you came alone. You realize that you can still enjoy yourself at the wedding and get to know other guests even while being alone.
PD comprehension question: Do you enjoy yourself at the wedding?

PD scenario body: Your favorite part of the day is winding down to eat dinner. When you start making dinner, you realize that you've been eating alone a lot recently, and you feel sad. You decide you are okay with this fact, as it gives you time to reflect on your day.
PD comprehension question: Are you okay with eating dinner alone?

PD scenario body: You have off on Monday for a holiday, and you know people will make plans for the long weekend. After a close friend said they're out of town for the weekend, you realize you don't have anyone to hang out with for the long weekend. This makes you optimistic about making new friends next week.
PD comprehension question: Are you sad about the long weekend?

PD scenario body: Your friends are planning a dinner for someone's birthday. You would have liked to have joined in, but your friends didn't include you. You feel insecure.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: You realize that you can have dinner by yourself and get your favorite meal. You feel confident.
PD comprehension question: Are you worried about going to dinner on your own?

PD scenario body: You go to see a movie on opening night. When you get to the theater, you notice that you are the only person sitting alone.  You feel excited for the movie to start.
PD comprehension question: Are you okay with sitting alone?

PD scenario body: You just moved to a new apartment and you are living alone for the first time in a while. Your days are very quiet and you often find yourself staying in on the weekends with much fewer plans  than you had when you were living with people. This feels like a relaxing change of pace.
PD comprehension question: Are you bored living alone?

PD scenario body: You are giving a presentation to your coworkers about a new training plan. You feel that your hands are shaking a little. Wondering if it is obvious, you look around and see that a few people have noticed.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: You realize your coworkers are more interested in your presentation than your hands. You continue your presentation, and as time passes, you discover that your nerves are manageable.
PD comprehension question: Are you able to handle your anxiety during your presentation?

PD scenario body: You are visiting your sister in a different city for an extended period of time. Your sister seems to know a lot of people in the area and is always out of the apartment, leaving you alone. You think it is nice that they are not home often because you prefer to be alone to relax at the end of the day.
PD comprehension question: Are you bothered by your sister's absence?

PD scenario body: You just moved to a new city and have met a lot of really interesting people. However, you feel like you still don't have a very close group of friends to rely on and miss your close friends from your old city. You decide to spend more time with the people you have met so you can become closer friends.
PD comprehension question: Are you optimistic when you think about your new friends?

PD scenario body: You have been taking a dance class at the local community center, and the time has come for your end of class showcase. You want to invite your friends and family to the showcase, but you worry you'll embarrass yourself if you are not very good. While deciding whether or not to invite people to your show, you see yourself bombing.
PD comprehension question: If you invite your friends and family, they will probably think
PD correct response: You looked really silly and hope they're not invited again.
PD incorrect response: You did a good job and they hope you invite them to future showcases.

PD scenario body: You go to send an email to your department. As you type the email, you wonder about the wording. You think of different ways to relay the information and feel overwhelmed.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: You decide to continue to draft the email and send it once you are happy with how it sounds. You look forward to people's responses.
PD comprehension question: Are you worried about sending a wordy email?

PD scenario body: You have recently joined a new department within your job.  When you go to the first team meeting, you notice that most people have other friends to sit with. As you take a seat alone, you realize that there is always an opportunity make new friendships as the year progresses.
PD comprehension question: Will there be future opportunities to make new friends?

PD scenario body: You make plans to go out to dinner with a friend, but you aren't sure if they've been around a large group of people recently. When they arrive to dinner, they keep taking sips of water, explaining that their throat tickles. You figure it's probably from talking a lot at dinner.
PD comprehension question: Do you think your friend's tingly throat is a sign of a cold virus?

PD scenario body: You recently visited a friend that you hadn’t seen in a long time, and you found out that your partner woke up with a sore throat when you got back. You are scared you may have accidentally exposed your friend to the same virus that your partner has. You think about what will happen if your friend gets sick and feel your friend will understand and forgive you.
PD comprehension question: Will your friend be angry at you for exposing her?

PD scenario body: You do not want to get on social media often. You have been pressured by several friends about getting on more. The thought of feeling left out makes you feel sad.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: You think about getting on social media more, and still decide not to. You feel relieved.
PD comprehension question: Do you feel anxious about your decision to not get on social media more often?

PD scenario body: You are at a crowded outdoor park. There are a lot of people, and you aren't sure if everyone is vaccinated. You think about how you're protected from the flu because you got vaccinated.
PD comprehension question: Are you vaccinated?

PD scenario body: You arrive to a support group meeting with your mask on. You notice that all of your peers are not wearing a mask. You think that they must be well.
PD comprehension question: Are any of the support group members sick?

PD scenario body: You have a cold, but you decide to go to the grocery store since you feel well enough. You can't help but cough a few times, and you worry that others are judging you. No one has noticed you coughing, so you feel better about your decision to go to the store.
PD comprehension question: Are you glad that you went to the grocery store?

PD scenario body: After being inactive for a few years, you recently joined a recreational trivia league. There is a tournament at the end of the season, and you are not sure how you will perform. Most likely, you will be successful.
PD comprehension question: Your performance will likely

PD scenario body: You are going to a potluck dinner party, and you want to make your family's macaroni casserole to bring. You have the recipe your parents gave you, but you worry you don't have the cooking skills to pull off the dish. You think that people at the party will probably think your casserole tastes unappetizing.
PD comprehension question: When you think about the reactions to your casserole, you see the other guests
PD correct response: Leaving their servings on their plates.

PD scenario body: You have been asked to talk about your journey with PD in your support group. This is a great opportunity to highlight the good work you have been doing, but you feel nervous talking in front of such a big group of your peers. As you think about how the people in the support group will react to your story, you believe they will be encouraging.
PD comprehension question: At the support group, people will probably think
PD correct response: You do a good job explaining your journey.
PD incorrect response: You look uncomfortable talking about your journey.

PD scenario body: While at a friend's house for a dinner party, your host mentions that you are a good piano player, and asks you to play something on the piano for the other guests. You know a few pieces you can play well, but you feel nervous playing on the spot without practice. Walking up to the piano, you think to yourself that everyone will enjoy it.
PD comprehension question: How do you think the other party guests will feel about your piano performance?
PD correct response: They will think you are talented.
PD incorrect response: They will think you need to practice more.

PD scenario body: You and your partner decide to spend time at the pool. You notice you forgot to pack sunscreen, even after your partner specifically told you to remember it. You think your partner will be angry.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: You apologize to your partner and volunteer to go get sunscreen somewhere. Your partner thanks you for the offer and is not upset.
PD comprehension question: Is your partner mad at you?

PD scenario body: You have been taking a dance class at the local community center, and the time has come for your end of class showcase. You want to invite your friends and family to the showcase, but you worry you'll embarrass yourself if you are not very good. While deciding whether or not to invite people to your show, you see yourself doing well.
PD comprehension question: Based on how you see yourself performing, inviting your friends and family is probably

PD scenario body: You are going to a potluck dinner party, and you want to make your family's macaroni casserole to bring. You have the recipe your parents gave you, but you worry you don't have the cooking skills to pull off the dish. You think that people at the party will probably think your casserole tastes great.
PD comprehension question: The other guests will probably think your casserole

PD scenario body: Tomorrow is a close friend's birthday. You forgot to get them a gift and don't have much time to shop for something good. You pick up something mediocre from a nearby store, and you think they will be appreciative.
PD comprehension question: You think that your friend is likely to think your gift is
PD correct response: A nice gesture on their birthday.

PD scenario body: You get assigned to a new project where you do not know anyone else. On the first day of meeting, you put yourself out there to try to make new friends. When you think about asking some colleagues if they want to grab drinks after work, you expect them to say yes.
PD comprehension question: If you ask your co-workers to get drinks after work, they will probably
PD correct response: Agree that getting drinks is a good idea.

PD scenario body: Your family shares one older car that has a big dent in the front. Today, you are driving it around town. When you think about people you know seeing your car, you feel nervous.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: As you're getting out of the car, you see a few acquaintances. They smile and wave at you, and you feel relieved.
PD comprehension question: Do your acquaintances judge your car?

PD scenario body: You have been very busy and are having trouble keeping in contact with one of your best friends. You are usually good about calling them, but your schedule has been very hectic. As you think about their reaction to this lull in contact, you think that your friend will be forgiving.
PD comprehension question: Your friend will most likely
PD correct response: Understand that sometimes things get very busy.
PD incorrect response: Be upset with you for not staying in contact.

PD scenario body: Tomorrow is a close friend's birthday. You forgot to get them a gift and don't have much time to shop for something good. You pick up something mediocre from a nearby store and you think they will be upset
PD comprehension question: You imagine that when you show up at your friend's birthday, they will think
PD correct response: "This is almost worse than no gift at all."
PD incorrect response: "Aw. It was really nice of my friend to bring me a gift."

PD scenario body: You arrive at a luncheon in a new outfit. Everyone turns to look at you as you walk in. You decide that they must think you look stylish.
PD comprehension question: Did everyone at the luncheon like your new outfit?

PD scenario body: You are outside walking and see a group of people ahead of you. As you pass them, you stumble slightly and feel a little foolish. You hear them laugh as you pass by and assume that they are laughing at jokes that aren't about you.
PD comprehension question: The group of walkers were probably laughing because ...
PD correct response: Someone told a funny joke that was not about you.

PD scenario body: Your friend is having a get together tonight. You weren't invited, but most of your other friends were. When you think about it, this makes you feel excluded.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: You realize you told your friends you had a lot of other things you needed to do tonight. This makes you feel relieved.
PD comprehension question: Were you not invited because you were busy?

PD scenario body: Your friend is going bowling tonight, and you didn't get an invite. Most of your other friends were invited, so you think you weren't invited due to your PD symptoms. When you think about it, you realize it's because you are busy.
PD comprehension question: Were you not invited because you were busy?

PD scenario body: You look through your social media feed and noticed that your grandkids only post pictures with their friends. You posted a family picture and tagged your grandkids. You asked your grandkids if they minded, and they said that it was a great photo.
PD comprehension question: Are your grandkids upset that you tagged them in a family photo?

PD scenario body: You're at a party, and some of the partygoers notice your symptoms and stare. You feel uncomfortable and decide not to engage in conversation with them. You confide in your close friend that is also at the party, and your friend thinks that your decision is justified.
PD comprehension question: Does your friend agree with you about not engaging with others?

PD scenario body: You are afraid of the wilderness. This weekend, you are going walking with friends and you know wild animals have been frequently spotted in the area. As you think about the outing, you feel scared.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: You think about the camping trip a little more and realize you're enjoying the experience too much to be scared. You feel calmer.
PD comprehension question: Will the trip be miserable?

PD scenario body: You are volunteering at a community event and notice two old friends who you have not seen in a while. They come up to say hi as they are passing. They probably think that you are selfless.
PD comprehension question: Do your friends think that you are kind for volunteering?

PD scenario body: You have recently joined a local book club and think the other members are very cool. You would like to hang out more often, but you worry about whether they will want to be friends outside of the book club. You consider inviting them over for dinner, and you think that they will think you are fun.
PD comprehension question: The other book club members probably
PD correct response: Will enjoy your company outside the book club.
PD incorrect response: Will not want to be friends outside of the book club.

PD scenario body: You have been very busy and are having trouble keeping in contact with one of your best friends. You are usually good about calling them, but your schedule has been very hectic. As you think about their reaction to this lull in contact, you think that your friend will be upset.
PD comprehension question: Since you have been out of touch, your friend is probably thinking
PD correct response: "I guess this friendship isn't worth very much."
PD incorrect response: "I'm sure things are just really busy right now."

PD scenario body: You live with a few extended family members. Your friend asks if they can come over for dinner one day, but they don't know how many people live in your home. As you picture your friend coming into your home, you think they will be surprised.
PD scenario body #2 (negation scenarios: You consider that you live in a different situation compared to your friend. You also know that your friend is really nice, so they will be accepting.
PD comprehension question: Does your friend judge you for living with lots of extended family members?

PD scenario body: Your significant other's children have invited you to join them for the holidays. You are excited to spend time with your partner's family, but you worry your own children will be disappointed that you are not with them. When you think about telling your children you would like to spend the holidays with your partner, you believe that they will be empathetic.
PD comprehension question: Your children are likely to think that you
PD correct response: Are just excited to spend the holidays with your partner, and not take it personally.
PD incorrect response: Don't care about them or want to see them.

PD scenario body: When you think about the next 10 years, you wonder which friends will still be a part of your life. You hope to keep in touch with your closest friends but know that it will be difficult. When you imagine yourself in 10 years, you see you and your close friends having stayed close.
PD comprehension question: You think that staying close to your current friends
PD correct response: Will be something that brings you happiness for years to come.
PD incorrect response: Is almost impossible in the long run.

PD scenario body: You and your best friend have known each other since college. You then worked in different fields and now have different interests, and you worry that you won't understand each other as you continue to get older. When you think about upcoming major life events, you expect your best friend to be supportive.
PD comprehension question: When you think about upcoming events in your life
PD correct response: You expect your best friend will try to understand what you're going through.
PD incorrect response: You think your best friend will not empathize with your experiences.
